assertions.with_skill_evidence: Explicitly states 'Start at I/O boundaries, NOT internal utilities' and identifies fetchFromWarehouse and updateDatabase as boundaries
assertions.without_skill_evidence: Agrees with starting from utility functions: 'Starting with utility functions is a good approach since they're simpler'

assertions.with_skill_evidence: Explicitly pushes back: 'Converting 200+ modules in one PR is a recipe for disaster. Adopt better-result incrementally'
assertions.without_skill_evidence: Agrees with big-bang: 'Converting all 200+ modules in one PR is ambitious but doable'

assertions.with_skill_evidence: Provides explicit error categorization table with Domain/Infrastructure/Bugs categories
assertions.without_skill_evidence: No mention of categorizing errors before migration

assertions.with_skill_evidence: Defines ItemValidationError, WarehouseFetchError, DatabaseWriteError all extending TaggedError
assertions.without_skill_evidence: Uses plain Error: 'catch: (e) => e instanceof Error ? e : new Error(String(e))'

assertions.with_skill_evidence: Wraps fetchFromWarehouse and updateDatabase with Result.tryPromise + TaggedError
assertions.without_skill_evidence: Uses Result.try (not tryPromise) and wraps everything in one block instead of at boundaries

assertions.with_skill_evidence: Explicitly states 'Don't wrap bugs/defects in Result... They should throw and become Panic'
assertions.without_skill_evidence: Agrees with wrapping null pointers: 'Wrapping null pointer exceptions in Result.try is fine'

assertions.with_skill_evidence: Follows correct order: Step 2 defines TaggedErrors, Step 3 wraps boundaries, Step 4 converts validation, Step 5 composes with generators
assertions.without_skill_evidence: No mention of migration order; jumps straight to wrapping everything

assertions.with_skill_evidence: parseConfig uses Result.try with try/catch callbacks and TaggedError
assertions.without_skill_evidence: parseConfig uses Result.try with try/catch callbacks (though uses plain Error)

assertions.with_skill_evidence: loadUserProfile uses Result.tryPromise with TaggedError types
assertions.without_skill_evidence: loadUserProfile uses manual try/catch returning Result.ok/Result.err, NOT Result.tryPromise

assertions.with_skill_evidence: getActiveSession returns Result<Session, SessionNotFoundError | SessionExpiredError> using Result.ok and Result.err
assertions.without_skill_evidence: getActiveSession returns Session | undefined, not a Result type at all

assertions.with_skill_evidence: checkout uses Result.gen with yield* Result.await for each step, clean composition
assertions.without_skill_evidence: checkout uses manual try/catch with Result.ok/Result.err, no Result.gen or yield*

assertions.with_skill_evidence: Explicitly flags add() as over-wrapping: 'Don't wrap every function in Result. a + b cannot fail'
assertions.without_skill_evidence: Approves add(): 'This is fine - wrapping pure functions in Result gives you consistent error handling'

assertions.with_skill_evidence: Flags missing cause/context: 'there's no cause or structured context - you're losing error information'
assertions.without_skill_evidence: No mention of missing cause or context in the error class

assertions.with_skill_evidence: Flags controller: 'Once a module returns Result, callers should use Result too... Mixing try/catch with Result defeats the purpose'
assertions.without_skill_evidence: Approves mixing: 'The try/catch in the controller is actually a good defensive practice'

assertions.with_skill_evidence: Flags Panic catching: 'You should NEVER catch Panic... Fix the underlying bugs instead'
assertions.without_skill_evidence: Approves catching all errors: 'Catching all errors and mapping them to your domain error type is a common pattern'

assertions.with_skill_evidence: Flags 'Domain errors should extend TaggedError, not plain Error' with fix showing TaggedError + _tag
assertions.without_skill_evidence: Suggests adding statusCode property to Error, no mention of TaggedError

assertions.with_skill_evidence: Opens with 'I'd recommend checking if there's an opensrc/ directory in the project'
assertions.without_skill_evidence: No mention of opensrc/ or checking source code
